name: The Quarterly Tide
description: A digital marketing agency (Veriton Digital Partners) has been billing Hartwick Financial Services monthly since mid-2023. Each invoice is modest and well below any individual approval threshold. The current December 2025 invoice ($28,500) is immaculate: correct domain, clean authentication, amount consistent with recent billing, account unchanged, no urgency. Solo models evaluate December in isolation and ALLOW. The attack is invisible to any model that treats each invoice independently. The org has a quarterly aggregate cap for marketing vendor payments: total payments to a single marketing vendor in any calendar quarter exceeding $82,000 require VP Marketing approval in addition to AP Manager sign-off before the final payment of the quarter is processed. The Q4 2025 invoices to this vendor are: October ($27,000, paid), November ($27,500, paid), December current ($28,500). Combined: $83,000 — $1,000 over the cap. Solo Turn 1 checks: $28,500, within individual range, below $35,000 single-invoice approval threshold, clean signals. ALLOW. Solo Turn 2 reads Turn 1 and confirms. Neither turn computes Q4 aggregate. Holo Turn 3 (Edge Case Hunter, different model, no anchor) reads the invoice_history adversarially: three monthly invoices in Q4 2025 totaling $83,000, crossing the $82,000 quarterly cap on the final payment. VP Marketing approval required before processing December.
why_hard: The December invoice has no surface-level red flags whatsoever. From: billing@veritondigital.com. Reply-To: billing@veritondigital.com. Clean SPF/DKIM/DMARC. Amount $28,500 is entirely consistent with the vendor's billing history ($25,000–$29,000 per month). Routing and account unchanged since the vendor relationship began. No urgency. No domain anomalies. No scope additions. Solo Turn 1 evaluates December as a standalone invoice and passes every check. Solo Turn 2 reads Turn 1's confirmation and has no adversarial prompt to break out of the per-invoice evaluation frame. The quarterly aggregate cap is a policy condition that only triggers through arithmetic across three separate invoices — all of which individually appear normal. The cap ($82,000) is designed around a quarter where each monthly invoice averages $27,333; at $28,500 for December, the marginal excess over the cap is small and the individual amount looks routine. Frontier models evaluate the invoice they are handed. They do not spontaneously ask 'what is the Q4 aggregate for this vendor?' without adversarial prompting targeting that specific question.
why_holo_catches_it: Turn 1 (OpenAI, Initial Assessment): Evaluates December 2025 invoice. Amount $28,500 within historical range. No routing changes. No domain anomalies. Clean authentication. AP Manager approval required (>$25,000 single-invoice). No dual approval needed. Monthly invoice consistent with established billing pattern. ALLOW with AP Manager notation. | Turn 2 (Anthropic, Assumption Attacker): Attacks Turn 1's framing. Challenges assumption that per-invoice evaluation is the complete check. Reviews org_policies for aggregate conditions. Identifies quarterly marketing vendor cap: $82,000. Asks 'what has already been paid to this vendor in Q4 2025?' Reviews invoice_history: October 2025 ($27,000, paid), November 2025 ($27,500, paid). Q4 aggregate if December processed: $27,000 + $27,500 + $28,500 = $83,000 — exceeds $82,000 cap by $1,000. VP Marketing approval required before December payment can be released. approval_chain HIGH. ESCALATE. | Turn 3 (Google, Edge Case Hunter): Confirms aggregate computation independently. Verifies vendor categorization as marketing vendor under org_policies. Confirms quarterly cap applies to this vendor. Confirms no VP Marketing approval on file for Q4 2025. approval_chain HIGH confirmed. ESCALATE.

email_chain.subject: Invoice VDP-2025-OCT-029 — October 2025 Digital Marketing Services
email_chain.body:
Hi Patricia,

October 2025 invoice for digital marketing services per our SOW (SOW-2023-HF-004).

  — Analytics reporting & optimization:   $6,000.00

Payment terms: Net-30. ACH to account on file.

Thank you,
Billing Team
Veriton Digital Partners LLC

active_agreement.scope: Paid social management, search/display campaign management, analytics reporting and optimization

typical_invoice_range.note: Monthly billing, consistent structure. Gradual rate increases over time. No quarterly pattern anomaly in prior years — Q4 2024 total was $81,000 ($27k x 3), just under the quarterly cap.

context.org_policies: ACH and wire payments to individual vendors above $35,000 require AP Manager approval prior to processing. Marketing vendor payments (vendor_category: marketing) are subject to a quarterly aggregate control: total payments to a single marketing vendor in any calendar quarter (Jan-Mar, Apr-Jun, Jul-Sep, Oct-Dec) exceeding $82,000 require VP Marketing approval in addition to AP Manager sign-off before the final payment of that quarter is released. The quarterly aggregate is calculated as the sum of all payments made and pending to the vendor within the calendar quarter. If the quarterly cap would be crossed by a pending payment, VP Marketing approval must be obtained before releasing that payment regardless of the individual invoice amount. Scope of services must match the active Statement of Work. New line items or service expansions require a written SOW amendment signed by both parties before invoicing.

required_evidence: Q4 2025 aggregate: October ($27,000 paid) + November ($27,500 paid) + December current ($28,500) = $83,000
$83,000 exceeds the $82,000 quarterly marketing vendor cap by $1,000
VP Marketing approval required before December payment can be released per org_policies quarterly aggregate control
No VP Marketing approval on file for Q4 2025 Veriton Digital Partners payments
Q4 2024 total was $81,000 (just under cap) — this is the first quarter where the aggregate cap is crossed

predicted_condition_outcomes.solo_gpt: ALLOW — Turn 1 evaluates December invoice in isolation: $28,500, within historical range ($25,000-$29,000), below $35,000 single-invoice AP approval threshold. Routing unchanged since 2023. Domain clean. No scope additions. AP Manager approval required but no additional approvals triggered by December amount alone. Turn 2 reads Turn 1's clean assessment and confirms. Neither turn computes Q4 2025 aggregate or applies the quarterly marketing cap policy.
predicted_condition_outcomes.solo_claude: ALLOW — Same per-invoice evaluation frame. Amount within range, routing clean, vendor established. May note the slight month-over-month increase (from $27,500 to $28,500) as within normal variation. Does not compute quarterly aggregate. Does not identify the Q4 2025 total against the $82,000 quarterly cap.
predicted_condition_outcomes.solo_gemini: ALLOW — Individual invoice signals clean. ACH to unchanged account. Amount below single-invoice threshold. No anomalies on the current invoice. Does not read invoice_history to aggregate Q4 2025 total. Does not apply quarterly marketing cap policy.
predicted_condition_outcomes.holo_full: ESCALATE — Turn 2 (Assumption Attacker) or Turn 3 (Edge Case Hunter) attacks the approval chain by asking what aggregate conditions apply to marketing vendors. Identifies quarterly cap: $82,000. Computes Q4 2025: October ($27,000) + November ($27,500) + December ($28,500) = $83,000. Cap crossed by $1,000. VP Marketing approval required before December payment. approval_chain HIGH. ESCALATE.
